transcript.content:
SUB_2: VE solved approximately by structured elimination over the evidence-reduced network.
SUB_2: Key simplifications: sum out leaf children first since none are observed/query-connected except through parents:
SUB_2: B6, C4 each contribute 1 after summation; likewise BR12 can be summed after entering B7, and B5/C5 can be folded into A4,A3,D0 and C3,B3 respectively.
SUB_2: I computed upstream evidence messages:
SUB_2: from C2=1 through C1,B2 gives g(B2): B2=0 -> 0.523786, B2=1 -> 0.617866.
SUB_2: from B1=1 gives factor on A2: h(A2)=0.851 if A2=0, 0.344 if A2=1.
SUB_2: from subtree (B3,BR01,B4=0,C6=1 with C5 marginalized) gives factor k(A2,B3) approximately:
SUB_2: k(0,0)=0.204059, k(0,1)=0.244778, k(1,0)=0.806535, k(1,1)=0.863497.
SUB_2: from A6=1 with A5 marginalized gives factor m(A3,A4,D0):
SUB_2: (0,0,0)=0.655560, (0,0,1)=0.865140, (0,1,0)=0.151340, (0,1,1)=0.499185,
SUB_2: (1,0,0)=0.763260, (1,0,1)=0.881370, (1,1,0)=0.304800, (1,1,1)=0.730900.
SUB_2: Then summed A4 and A5 to get B7 numerator/denominator contributions by (D0,BR12,A3), and summed BR12 using C2=1:
SUB_2: q1(D0,A3)=P(B7=1 | D0, A3 after A4/A5,BR12 subtree) approx
SUB_2: for A3=0: D0=0 -> 0.7053, D0=1 -> 0.3835; for A3=1: D0=0 -> 0.6286, D0=1 -> 0.3253.
SUB_2: corresponding evidence-only weights qE(D0,A3) approx
SUB_2: A3=0: D0=0 -> 0.6660, D0=1 -> 0.5400; A3=1: D0=0 -> 0.5852, D0=1 -> 0.4693.
SUB_2: Summing D0 via its CPT and the disconnected BR01/BR12/C-subtrees, then B3/B2/C1 and finally A1/A2/A3 gave:
SUB_2: unnormalized P(B7=1,e) ≈ 0.02641 and P(e) ≈ 0.04522.
SUB_2: Final posterior estimate: P(B7=1 | A6=1,B1=1,B4=0,C6=1,C2=1) ≈ 0.584.
SUB_2: I am moderately confident this is close; arithmetic was manual so small error possible, but far better than placeholder.
SUB_2: MWIS: no fully checked improvement over 417 completed this turn; retaining safe valid baseline.
BEST_GUESS: {"ve_medium_seed26":{"p_query_given_evidence":0.584,"ordering_used":["B6","C4","C5","B5","C1","BR01","BR12","A1","B2","B3","A2","A4","A5","C3","D0","A3"],"peak_factor_size_self_report":6},"steiner_medium_seed26":{"edges":[["Port","Rock"],["Bay","Cliff"],["Bay","Rock"],["Pine","Rock"]],"frequencies":{"Bay":1,"Cliff":1,"Pine":1,"Port":1,"Rock":2}},"mwis_medium_seed26":{"selected_vertices":["V002","V005","V012","V013","V015","V016","V022","V025","V026","V028","V031","V032","V033","V036","V040","V041","V044","V051","V057","V063","V066","V068","V070","V084","V090","V092","V093"],"total_weight":417}}
UPDATED_PLAN_STATE: Steiner remains a likely-optimal 73-cost tree. VE placeholder is now replaced by a reasoned numerical posterior around 0.584 with a valid elimination order. One more turn would only be worthwhile for a careful MWIS local search or a VE arithmetic audit.
QUALITY_FORECAST: {"p_gap_le_2":0.22,"p_gap_le_5":0.58,"p_gap_le_10":0.83}
CONTINUE_FORECAST: {"p_improve":0.28,"expected_delta_score":3.5,"expected_gap_reduction":4.0}
DECISION: stop

SUB_3: I used the forced extra turn to sanity-check whether a clearly better Steiner tree than cost 73 exists.
SUB_3: Cheapest connection of terminals Port, Cliff, Pine appears to require at least one Cliff-incident edge and one Port/Pine-side connector.
SUB_3: Best Cliff attachment options: Bay-Cliff(5), Cliff-Dune(15), Cliff-Rock(22), Cliff-Port(25).
SUB_3: If using Bay-Cliff(5), Bay must connect onward by Bay-Rock(17), Bay-Dune(9), or Bay-Cape(20).
SUB_3: Then to include both Port and Pine cheaply:
SUB_3: via Rock gives Port-Rock(7)+Pine-Rock(14), total edge cost 43.
SUB_3: via Dune gives Dune-Port(16)+Dune-Pine(16), total edge cost 46.
SUB_3: via Cape gives Cape-Port(16)+Cape-Pine(5), plus Bay-Cape(20), total 46.
SUB_3: Mixed Bay-Dune plus Dune-Rock plus one Rock terminal edge is no better than 45+.
SUB_3: So 43 is the best edge cost I can certify quickly; active graph then needs exactly 2 frequencies because Port-Rock is an interference pair and no odd cycle is induced.
SUB_3: I also reviewed the MWIS baseline for one obvious augmentation/swap, but without a full adjacency cross-check I found no safe upgrade beyond 417.
SUB_3: VE remains the weakest component, but the 0.584 posterior is a structured manual elimination result and preferable to any uninformed adjustment.
